retry_reason: The put operation is unconditional when called on non-NULL; if the refcount were already zero, retrying would cause a double-put. If the pointer is NULL, retrying won't change that.
contract_summary: This is a reference-release (put) wrapper: it decrements the reference count of the hive object by calling kobject_put on its embedded kobj. After calling this function with a non-NULL hive pointer, the caller must not dereference the hive unless it holds another valid reference. The function is void and requires no return value check. It is safe to call with NULL (no-op). No locking or RCU requirements are imposed by this wrapper itself.

judge.reason: Function's sole purpose is to release a reference on a hive object via kobject_put; it contains only a NULL check and the put call.

check.reason: kobject_put is called on &hive->kobj, which is the kobject member of the parameter hive. The access path from parameter 1 (hive) to the refcount is hive.kobj.kref.refcount, and the type chain amdgpu_hive_info--kobject--kref--refcount_t matches the actual struct definitions. The segment count is consistent (4 segments, 4 types). The direction 'put' and the location (parameter 1) are correct.
